components.name: ConfettiHost
components.summary: A one-shot celebration burst — deliberately sparse and slow so it reads as a *signal*, not a party store.

agentMeta.extend: Add defaulted params only. Pass a custom colors list to tint a burst to one data domain; otherwise it uses the four brand hues (PulseConfettiColors).
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
'conditional' perfTier: renders nothing until play=true, then emits one 200ms burst. Do not leave play=true (it re-emits on recomposition) — drive it from a one-shot event and reset it.
Do not fire on routine actions. Confetti is a signal reserved for genuine milestones (a PR, a goal hit, onboarding complete), not every save.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: CelebrationPulse
components.summary: A soft expanding ring-glow behind content — the quieter celebration for badges and summary checkmarks, where full ConfettiHost confetti would be too loud.

agentMeta.extend: Wrap the thing being celebrated (a badge, a checkmark) as content. Pass a channel Color to tint the glow to a data domain; it defaults to the app's lead accent (Pulse.accent).
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
This self-animates on an infinite transition — it is the quiet celebration. Use it for a sustained badge glow, not a one-shot burst (that is ConfettiHost).
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: HeatCalendar
components.summary: A contribution-graph heat calendar: intensities is one value per day in chronological order, laid out in columns of 7 (a week per column).

agentMeta.extend: Pass intensities as one value per day in chronological order; it lays them out in columns of 7. Cell alpha scales over max (series max when null). Bind meaning with a channel.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
This is a pure Canvas draw with no self-animation. Do not push date/label semantics in — it only knows a chronological list of magnitudes.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: PulseBarChart
components.summary: A vertical bar chart with x-axis labels — magnitudes over a small set of periods (a training week, a spend-by-category row).

agentMeta.extend: Vertical bars for a small set of periods, with x labels. Bars grow in on the DATA motion the first time they compose (and re-grow when values change). The last bar is emphasized; pass emphasizeLast=false to level them.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
For a tiny inline series with no labels use Sparkline(asBars=true) instead — this is the full labelled version.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: PulseLineChart
components.summary: A line chart with an area fill and a draw-in reveal (the line wipes in left→right on the DATA spec).

agentMeta.extend: A trend line with area fill that wipes in left-to-right on the DATA motion. Min-max normalized by default (fills the height); pass normalizeMinMax=false for a zero baseline.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
For a tiny inline trend use Sparkline (line mode) instead — this is the full-height version with the draw-in reveal.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: HeroPanel
components.summary: The hero panel — a gradient-filled greeting/CTA surface, the app's lead voice at the top of a screen.

agentMeta.extend: The gradient greeting/CTA surface. Defaults to structure.heroGradient (the accent's lead voice); pass structure.energyGradient for the celebration voice, or any Brush. Content is a ColumnScope.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
Hero text sits on a gradient, not the surface scheme — set content colors for the gradient (typically light/on-gradient) rather than relying on onSurface.
Do not hand-build the gradient from literal colors — use structure.heroGradient / structure.energyGradient so it tracks the app accent.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.

components.name: OnboardingScaffold
components.summary: The intro-pager shell: pageCount swipeable pages with a PulsePageIndicator, a "Skip" text action, and a primary PulseButton that advances ("Next") until the last page, where it reads "Done" and calls onFinish.

agentMeta.extend: The intro-pager shell (pager + PulsePageIndicator + Skip + Next/Done). Supply each page via the page slot (usually an OnboardingPage) and put finish behavior (persist 'seen', navigate) in onFinish.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.

Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
Do not put onboarding state/persistence into Pulse — the scaffold is presentation only; the 'has seen onboarding' flag and page content live app-side.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: TickerNumber
components.summary: A DataText readout that rolls up to target when it first appears (and sweeps between values on change), on the slower data easing so the number feels measured rather than printed.

components.name: StatTile
components.summary: A stat tile: caption + big mono value (+ optional unit), tinted by its channel.

agentMeta.extend: Add new parameters only, each with a backward-compatible default, so existing call sites compile and render pixel-identically. Never change or remove an existing parameter's default.

'conditional' perfTier: this tile is static UNLESS you pass animatedValue (rolls via TickerNumber) or sparkline. Prefer the static form unless a live metric genuinely needs motion.
The dense metric layout is opt-in via dense=true; leaving it false must stay pixel-identical to the standard tile.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: Sparkline
components.summary: A minimal sparkline / bar series.

agentMeta.extend: Add new parameters only, each with a backward-compatible default, so existing call sites compile and render pixel-identically. Never change or remove an existing parameter's default.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.

Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
Do NOT assume Sparkline self-animates — it is a pure Canvas draw with no internal animation. Any motion comes from the caller changing the values list.
asBars=true scales bars to the series max from a zero baseline; asBars=false draws a line (min-max normalized by default). Pass a non-null strokeWidth for the filled line-chart mode.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.

components.name: PulseSegmentedControl
components.summary: A segmented control — a hairline-bordered pill split into 2–4 choices, the selected one filled with the channel's dim tone and channel-colored text.

agentMeta.extend: A 2-4 way exclusive choice (the Dark/Light/System appearance switch is built from it). String labels + selectedIndex + onSelect(index); map your enum to labels app-side. Pass a channel to bind the selection to a data domain.
neverDo: Fork or copy this component into a consumer app — in-tree drift is the exact failure Pulse exists to kill; extend the shared component instead.
Add app-domain names or meaning (protein, streak, heat, calories, macro...) to Pulse. Pulse knows hues and structure, never meaning.
Hard-code a hue — colors come from the accent channel (Pulse.accent) or an explicit channel/color parameter.
Use elevation or drop shadows for depth. Pulse depth is stroke + tone (hairline + panel/panelHigh), never shadow.
Keep it to a small number of segments — for many options use a list of PulseSelectableCard instead.
The selected fill is the channel dim tone; do not hard-code it.
agentMeta.meaningVia: Bind a surface to a data domain by passing a channel Color; the mapping of hue -> meaning lives app-side in a CompositionLocal layered over PulseTheme, not in Pulse.
